response:
Sec. 1  
Allergies to peanuts are immune system reactions to proteins found in peanuts  
These reactions can cause symptoms such as hives swelling difficulty breathing  
Living underwater is not related to these symptoms  
People who are allergic to peanuts can live underwater if they avoid peanut exposure  
The environment underwater does not influence the body's immune response  
Allergies are determined by the body's immune system not by external conditions  

Sec. 2  
The question may be based on a misunderstanding of what causes allergies  
Allergic reactions occur when the immune system mistakenly identifies a substance as harmful  
This process is internal and not affected by external environments such as water  
People who are allergic to peanuts can live in any environment including underwater  
They must still avoid peanuts and follow medical advice to manage their condition  
The inability to live underwater is not a consequence of peanut allergies
